chunk_summary: This section discusses the selection and use of WHO/IAP growth charts, emphasizing error prevention in plotting and measurement to ensure accurate growth monitoring in children.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of selecting the correct growth chart based on age, sex, and index.
Identify common plotting and measurement errors and strategies to prevent them.
Learn techniques to improve data quality and reduce false referrals in growth monitoring.
Recognize the role of digital tools and staff training in enhancing measurement reliability.
key_facts: Use sex-specific and age-appropriate charts: WHO (0–5 years) and IAP (5–18 years).
Common errors include age miscalculation, transcription mistakes (e.g., 6.2 kg vs. 6.02 kg), and unit confusion (cm vs. m).
Always plot in real time, record numeric values, and re-measure implausible centile jumps or drops.
Failing to measure and plot length in children under 2 years delays stunting recognition.
Digital charting systems can reduce errors if biologically implausible inputs are flagged for recheck.
Periodic staff training, checklists (e.g., shoes off, scale tared), and parent engagement improve reliability.
reference_quote: RCPCH: UK-WHO Growth Charts Guidance for Health Professionals — 'The most useful systems flag biologically implausible inputs and prompt immediate recheck.'
document_key_topics: Strategies to improve data quality in growth monitoring
Role of digital tools and staff training in error prevention
